name: sql-like-scatter-followup
goal: Reduce the remaining SQL-like scatter chart overhead after the 2026-04-01 profile isolated the gap to reflection-heavy source-row chart mapping.

sharedContext.summary: The 2026-04-01 warmed scatter profile kept direct and bound SQL-like scatter near parity, so bind/setup reuse is no longer the main issue. Versus fluent scatter, SQL-like scatter still measured 1.470x/1.340x/1.872x latency and 1.426x/1.566x/3.406x allocation at sizes 1000/10000/100000. The remaining gap is in the direct source-row chart path, with the hottest stack frames in ChartMapper.readField, ReflectionUtil.getFieldValue, ReflectionUtil.resolveFieldPath, and ChartMapper.MultiSeriesAccumulator.
constraints: Keep work bounded to the SQL-like scatter follow-up and the benchmark diagnostics needed to verify it.
Do not edit TODO.md, ai/state files, ai/log files, or ai/indexes.
Do not schedule parallel workers that edit the same files.
Preserve chart semantics for BAR, LINE, PIE, AREA, and SCATTER while optimizing the typed source-row path.
validation: java -jar target/*-benchmarks.jar 'laughing.man.commits.benchmark.ChartVisualizationJmhBenchmark.(fluentScatterMapping|sqlLikeScatterMapping|sqlLikeBoundScatterMapping)$' -p size=1000,10000,100000 -f 1 -wi 3 -i 5 -w 200ms -r 200ms -prof gc -rf json -rff target/benchmarks/<run>/scatter-gc.json

tasks.title: Analyze the chart fast-path shape
tasks.prompt: Inspect the 2026-04-01 scatter profile and the current chart/runtime code paths. Recommend the smallest runtime change that removes reflection-heavy typed source-row chart reads from the direct SQL-like scatter path. Be explicit about which existing array/query-row/flat-read-plan helpers should be reused and which semantics must remain unchanged.
constraints: Keep the recommendation scoped to the direct SQL-like chart path and reusable chart-mapper internals.
validation: Summarize the proposed runtime change and the exact methods to touch.

tasks.title: Design the benchmark isolation slice
tasks.prompt: Inspect the scatter benchmark, the 2026-04-01 profile artifacts, and the benchmarking guide. Propose a focused JMH slice that can distinguish chart-mapper typed-source overhead from SQL-like execution/setup overhead for scatter charts. Return concrete benchmark method additions or command changes and the exact validation command to run them.
constraints: Keep the proposal benchmark-only; do not propose runtime edits in this task.
validation: Summarize the proposed benchmark additions and the command needed to run them.

tasks.title: Implement the chart fast-path change
tasks.prompt: Implement the smallest coherent runtime optimization that reduces reflection-heavy typed source-row chart mapping for the direct SQL-like scatter path. Prefer reusing existing flat-read-plan, array-row, or indexed-row machinery instead of adding a SQL-like-only special case. Preserve the current direct-vs-bound scatter parity contract while adding or updating focused chart and SQL-like integration tests that protect the behavior.
constraints: Do not edit benchmark-module files in this task.
Preserve existing chart semantics and SQL-like scatter stability guarantees.
Keep direct and bound SQL-like scatter on the same optimized mapping path unless a test-proven reason requires divergence.

tasks.title: Add the scatter diagnostic benchmark slice
tasks.prompt: Add a focused benchmark slice that makes the remaining scatter overhead easier to separate into chart-mapper typed-source work versus SQL-like execution/setup work. Keep the change isolated to the benchmark/docs layer and align it with the current benchmark conventions and artifact naming.
constraints: Do not edit pojo-lens runtime files in this task.
Keep benchmark additions concrete, reproducible, and narrowly scoped to scatter diagnostics.

tasks.title: Review the scatter follow-up package
tasks.prompt: Review the runtime and benchmark changes together. Prioritize regressions, semantic drift, and validation gaps. Explicitly answer whether the runtime change really targets the reflection-heavy scatter cost identified in the 2026-04-01 profile and whether the new benchmark slice is strong enough to prove it.
constraints: Findings first; focus on runtime risk, benchmark quality, and missing validation.
